steps.title: Identify the timing gap
steps.content: The passage contrasts the impact believers' hope with a crucial problem: Kjær's team cannot determine the crater's age. The debate hinges on whether an impact at the Younger Dryas' start caused the cooling. Without dating the crater, no connection to that specific moment can be proven.

steps.title: Apply the timing gap
steps.content: A is the only choice that logically follows: without the crater's age, the team cannot confirm it caused the Younger Dryas' cooling at the period's onset—the exact connection the debate requires. B addresses a different question (whether comet fragments can make large craters), while C and D introduce unsupported claims. That matches choice A — A is correct.
